question: [File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setRoleSetter] Can an unprivileged caller (non-roleSetter) call setRoleSetter(address(0)) under the precondition that roleSetter is already address(0) (e.g., after a prior setRoleSetter(address(0)) call), triggering a permanent lock of all four role-management functions (setFeeSetter, setFeeClaimer, setTickSpacingSetter, setRoleSetter), violating the invariant that protocol roles must remain reassignable, causing scoped impact: permanent freeze of all fee and tick-spacing governance? Proof idea: fuzz initial roleSetter address, call setRoleSetter(address(0)) as roleSetter, then assert every subsequent setFeeSetter/setFeeClaimer/setTickSpacingSetter/setRoleSetter call reverts with OnlyRoleSetter().

[File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setRoleSetter] Can the current roleSetter call setRoleSetter(newRoleSetter) twice within a single multicall batch under the precondition that multicall uses delegatecall and roleSetter is updated after the first inner call, triggering the second call to succeed with a different newRoleSetter than intended, violating the invariant that only the pre-call roleSetter can authorize role changes, causing scoped impact: unauthorized role reassignment within a single transaction? Proof idea: construct multicall([setRoleSetter(A), setRoleSetter(B)]) as original roleSetter and assert final roleSetter == B, not A.

[File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setFeeSetter] Can any address other than roleSetter call setFeeSetter(attacker) under the precondition that feeSetter != roleSetter, triggering a successful feeSetter reassignment, violating the invariant require(msg.sender == roleSetter, OnlyRoleSetter()), causing scoped impact: unauthorized control over all settlement and continuous fee parameters? [File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setFeeClaimer] Can any address other than roleSetter call setFeeClaimer(attacker) under the precondition that feeClaimer holds accumulated claimableSettlementFee > 0, triggering a feeClaimer reassignment that causes the old claimer to permanently lose access to unclaimed fees, violating the invariant that fee claimer transitions must not silently forfeit accrued protocol fees, causing scoped impact: permanent loss of claimable settlement fees for the displaced claimer? [File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketSettlementFee] Can feeSetter call setMarketSettlementFee(id, index, newSettlementFee) with newSettlementFee > maxSettlementFee(index) under the precondition that index is valid (0-6) and market exists, triggering a settlement fee above the ConstantsLib cap (e.g., MAX_SETTLEMENT_FEE_360_DAYS = 0.005e18), violating the invariant require(newSettlementFee <= maxSettlementFee(index), SettlementFeeTooHigh()), causing scoped impact: settlement fee exceeding protocol cap, implicitly cancelling all low-price offers? [File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketSettlementFee] Can feeSetter call setMarketSettlementFee(id, index, newSettlementFee) with newSettlementFee % CBP != 0 (CBP = 1e12) under the precondition that newSettlementFee <= maxSettlementFee(index), triggering a fee value that is not a multiple of centi-basis-points, violating the invariant require(newSettlementFee % CBP == 0, FeeNotMultipleOfFeeCbp()), causing scoped impact: settlement fee stored as truncated cbp value, creating rounding discrepancy between stored and intended fee? [File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketSettlementFee] Can feeSetter call setMarketSettlementFee(id, index, newSettlementFee) with index == 7 under the precondition that the market exists, triggering an out-of-bounds array access in maxSettlementFee(index) which indexes a fixed-size 7-element array, violating the invariant require(index <= 6, InvalidFeeIndex()), causing scoped impact: revert or silent array OOB depending on Solidity version, potentially bypassing fee cap enforcement? [File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setDefaultSettlementFee] Can feeSetter call setDefaultSettlementFee(loanToken, index, newSettlementFee) for a loanToken that already has active markets under the precondition that those markets were created with the old default fee, triggering a default fee change that affects only newly created markets (not existing ones), violating the invariant that market parameters are immutable after creation, causing scoped impact: new markets for the same loanToken silently receive different fee schedules than existing markets, creating fee arbitrage? [File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketContinuousFee] Can feeSetter call setMarketContinuousFee(id, newContinuousFee) with newContinuousFee > MAX_CONTINUOUS_FEE (MAX_CONTINUOUS_FEE = uint32(0.01e18 / 365 days) = 317097919) under the precondition that the market exists, triggering a continuous fee above the 1% annualized cap, violating the invariant require(newContinuousFee <= MAX_CONTINUOUS_FEE, ContinuousFeeTooHigh()), causing scoped impact: new lenders entering after the fee change receive a higher-than-capped continuous fee crystallized into their pendingFee? [File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketContinuousFee] Can feeSetter call setMarketContinuousFee(id, newContinuousFee) after lenders have already entered positions under the precondition that existing lenders have pendingFee crystallized at the old continuousFee rate, triggering a fee change that does NOT retroactively update existing lenders' pendingFee (per the comment 'their fee is fixed'), violating the invariant that fee changes must not silently alter existing lenders' crystallized obligations, causing scoped impact: new lenders entering after the change pay a different rate than existing lenders, creating accounting divergence in continuousFeeCredit? [File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketTickSpacing] Can tickSpacingSetter call setMarketTickSpacing(id, newTickSpacing) with newTickSpacing that is NOT a divisor of the current tickSpacing under the precondition that marketState[id].tickSpacing == 4 (DEFAULT_TICK_SPACING), triggering a tick spacing change to e.g. 3 (not a divisor of 4), violating the invariant require(marketState[id].tickSpacing % newTickSpacing == 0, InvalidTickSpacing()), causing scoped impact: invalid tick grid that could make previously valid offer ticks inaccessible? [File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketTickSpacing] Can tickSpacingSetter call setMarketTickSpacing(id, newTickSpacing) with newTickSpacing > current tickSpacing (e.g., newTickSpacing = 8 when current = 4) under the precondition that the market exists, triggering an increase in tick spacing that would make previously valid offer ticks inaccessible, violating the invariant that tick spacing can only decrease (require(marketState[id].tickSpacing % newTickSpacing == 0)), causing scoped impact: existing offers at ticks that are multiples of 4 but not 8 become unfillable? [File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketTickSpacing] Can tickSpacingSetter decrease tick spacing from DEFAULT_TICK_SPACING (4) to 1 under the precondition that existing offers are signed at tick multiples of 4, triggering the unlocking of ticks 1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 7, etc., violating the invariant that offer ticks must be multiples of the market's spacing at fill time (not at signing time), causing scoped impact: takers can now fill offers at ticks that were inaccessible when the offer was signed, potentially at prices the maker did not intend to offer?
